description_long: This is a narrow, multi-story brick apartment building with a distinctively European appearance, featuring ornate stonework and a steep, dark mansard roof. The ground floor houses an Irish pub, marked by green signage and large windows, while the upper floors have symmetrically arranged sash windows with decorative lintels. The facade is primarily composed of red and tan bricks, with horizontal stone bands separating each floor. The building sits on a stone-paved sidewalk, and the entrance to the pub is located at the corner, inviting foot traffic from the street. The overall structure is tall and slender, with no visible balconies and a strong vertical emphasis.
description: A tall, narrow brick apartment building with ornate stonework, a mansard roof, and an Irish pub on the ground floor. Symmetrical windows and decorative details give it a classic European look.
description_view_0: This view shows the side and partial front of the building, highlighting the brickwork, corner pub entrance, and multiple windows.
description_view_1: The front view displays the Irish pub entrance, green signage, and the symmetrical arrangement of windows above.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the opposite side of the building, with more brickwork and windows, and a clear view of the sidewalk.
description_view_3: The rear view shows a plain brick facade with no windows or entrances, emphasizing the building's verticality.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns on each facade, with even spacing and consistent alignment across all floors.
